recommendation.justification: CRT products are stable within the 15-25 °C range. Standard non-insulated packaging is sufficient when ambient conditions remain compatible throughout the route.

rules.name: CRT with extreme climate, short duration

recommendation.justification: Per GDP guidelines, CRT products exposed to ambient temperatures outside 15-25 °C require basic passive insulation to prevent excursions, even on short routes.

rules.name: CRT with extreme climate, long duration

recommendation.justification: Prolonged exposure (>120h) to ambient conditions outside the CRT range exceeds the protective capacity of passive systems. Active thermal management ensures stability for the full transit duration.
Per GDP guidelines, Cool (2-8 °C) products in tropical climates for under 48h can be protected by qualified passive shippers with conditioned gel packs.
Cool products on 48-72h routes in warm climates require premium passive shippers (PCM-based or VIP-insulated) qualified for the full transit window with a safety margin.
Per GDP guidelines, Cool products require active thermal management for transits >72h to maintain the 2-8 °C range reliably across varying ambient conditions.

rules.name: Cool, very long duration, planned recharges

recommendation.justification: Transits exceeding 120h for Cool products require active containers with planned recharge or battery-swap points to guarantee uninterrupted temperature control end-to-end.
Frozen products on short routes can be protected by qualified passive shippers using dry ice as the refrigerant, provided sublimation rates are validated for the transit window.
Frozen products on 48-96h routes require premium passive shippers with sufficient dry ice mass and high-performance insulation to maintain -20 to -15 °C without active intervention.
Per GDP guidelines, Frozen products on transits exceeding 96h require active refrigerated containers, as dry ice sublimation makes passive solutions unreliable at this duration.
UltraCold products on very short transits (<24h) may be shipped in ultra-premium passive shippers (deep-dry-ice or LN2 dry-vapor dewars) validated to hold -80 to -60 °C for the full window.
Per GDP guidelines, UltraCold products on any transit beyond 24h require active containers with planned dry ice or liquid nitrogen replenishment to maintain -80 to -60 °C reliably.
